claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have any children under 16 years old, or children aged 16-19 who are still in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I have two kids - Alex who is 8 years old, and Blake who is 9. They both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to know you have Alex (age 8) and Blake (age 9), and that they both live with you.

To help assess your eligibility, I need to understand a bit more about your living situation:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to ask about your immigration status, as this can affect eligibility:

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have any specific immigration status such as settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, pre-settled status, or another visa type?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. That makes things straightforward on that front.

Now, to ensure you meet the eligibility requirements, I need to confirm:

**Are you responsible for both Alex and Blake? This means either you live with them (which you've confirmed you do) or you're contributing financially towards their upkeep such as food, clothes, or pocket money?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for both of them - they live with me and I look after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that. Just to complete my assessment, I have one more important question:

**Is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake? In other words, are both children only claimed by you, or is another person (such as their other parent) also receiving Child Benefit for them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me:
- You are a British citizen living in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for both Alex (age 8) and Blake (age 9)
- Both children live with you
- You are the only person claiming Child Benefit for them

You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children. Let me record this outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake!**

You meet all the requirements:
- ✓ Both children are under 16
- ✓ You are responsible for them and live with them
- ✓ You live in the UK
- ✓ Only you are claiming (no one else is claiming for these children)

You can proceed with your claim for Child Benefit. If you need to make a claim or have any other questions about the process, you can contact HMRC or visit the relevant government website for guidance on how to apply.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Alex and is the sole person claiming Child Benefit for this child. All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, the claimant lives in the UK, they live together, and only one person is claiming.

Blake is 9 years old and lives with the claimant in the UK. The claimant is responsible for Blake and is the sole person claiming Child Benefit for this child. All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, the claimant lives in the UK, they live together, and only one person is claiming.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in Manchester, UK, with two children (Alex, age 8, and Blake, age 9) under their care and responsibility. Both children live with the claimant, and no other person is claiming Child Benefit for either child. The claimant meets all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children: they are under 16, the claimant is responsible for them, they live in the UK together, and only one person is claiming per child as required by the rules.

All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 16, the claimant lives in the UK, they live together, and only one person is claiming.

The claimant meets all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children: they are under 16, the claimant is responsible for them, they live in the UK together, and only one person is claiming per child as required by the rules.
